name: National Faith  Homebuyers
description_short: Non-profit housing counselor providing free homebuyer education, down payment assistance, and foreclosure prevention to first-time homebuyers in Michigan and Georgia.
description_long:
National Faith HomeBuyers was founded in 1996 by Dina Harris in Washtenaw County, Michigan, as a response to the founder's own need for homebuying guidance. Originally the Washtenaw Homebuyers Program, it relocated to Detroit and has since expanded to serve Detroit, Westland (Michigan), and Atlanta (Georgia). The organization operates as a non-profit with a mission to expand homeownership access to underserved populations who face barriers to entry in the housing market.

The organization offers a comprehensive suite of free services including homebuyer education classes, down payment assistance programs (up to $25,000 in Detroit), foreclosure prevention workshops, foreclosure assistance, financial literacy training, credit education, and HUD-approved housing counseling. They conduct virtual workshops via Eventbrite and host in-person information sessions and Fast Track workshops monthly. All services are explicitly stated to be free of charge.

National Faith HomeBuyers distinguishes itself through direct founder involvement (Dina Harris has 40+ years of housing industry experience) and a demonstrated track record: since 1996, they have counseled over 7,000 families and facilitated over 1,000 first-time home purchases. The organization emphasizes compassionate, non-judgmental service delivery and maintains a laser focus on first-time homebuyers and foreclosure prevention rather than offering broader financial services.

As a legitimate non-profit with operational longevity and documented impact, National Faith HomeBuyers is reliable for free housing counseling. The main limitation is geographic coverage—they only serve Michigan (Detroit/Westland) and Georgia (Atlanta), so availability is restricted. Additionally, while they provide down payment assistance, the scope and eligibility criteria are not fully detailed on their website.
pros: Completely free services—no fees for counseling, workshops, or education
Down payment assistance program offering up to $25,000 in Detroit
HUD-approved housing counseling with 7,000+ families counseled since 1996
Founder has 40+ years of direct housing industry experience
Documented success: over 1,000 first-time home purchases facilitated
Comprehensive offerings including foreclosure prevention and financial literacy
Monthly virtual and in-person workshops accessible via Eventbrite calendar
cons: Limited geographic footprint: only serves Detroit, Westland, and Atlanta—no national coverage
Down payment assistance eligibility criteria and application process not detailed on website
No online application system visible; requires phone calls or contact form submission
Mandatory attendance at information session before housing counseling appointments limits flexibility
No information provided about wait times or appointment availability
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Detroit, Westland, or Atlanta seeking free education and counseling
Homeowners facing foreclosure who need prevention counseling and assistance options
Low-to-moderate income families seeking down payment assistance and financial literacy training
Individuals with no prior homebuying experience who need step-by-step process guidance
diagnosis: National Faith HomeBuyers is ideal for first-time homebuyers and foreclosure-threatened homeowners in Detroit, Westland, or Atlanta who need free, comprehensive housing counseling and education. The organization is legitimate and well-established, but geographic limitation is the primary caveat—this service is not available outside Michigan and Georgia.
services: Down payment assistance program (up to $25,000 in Detroit)
Resources including applications, checklists, and educational materials
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
